name: GDPR
description: EU General Data Protection Regulation — core privacy and data protection obligations

requirements.requirement_text: Personal data shall be processed lawfully, fairly, and in a transparent manner.
requirements.expected_evidence: Privacy policy, lawful basis documentation, transparency notices
requirements.recommendation_text: Document lawful basis for each processing activity and publish clear privacy notices.

requirements.requirement_text: Personal data shall be collected for specified, explicit, and legitimate purposes and not further processed incompatibly.
requirements.expected_evidence: Data processing register, purpose statements in privacy policy
requirements.recommendation_text: Maintain a record of processing purposes and restrict secondary use without new legal basis.

requirements.requirement_text: Processing shall be lawful only if and to the extent that a valid legal basis applies (consent, contract, legal obligation, etc.).
requirements.expected_evidence: Legal basis assessment, consent records, LIAs
requirements.recommendation_text: Map each processing activity to an Article 6 legal basis with supporting documentation.

requirements.requirement_text: Where consent is the legal basis, the controller shall demonstrate that the data subject has consented to processing.
requirements.expected_evidence: Consent logs, consent UI screenshots, withdrawal mechanism
requirements.recommendation_text: Implement auditable consent capture with easy withdrawal equal to granting.

requirements.requirement_text: The controller shall provide transparent, intelligible, and easily accessible information about data processing to data subjects.
requirements.expected_evidence: Published privacy policy, layered notices, FAQ
requirements.recommendation_text: Publish layered privacy notices covering identity, purposes, rights, and retention.

requirements.requirement_text: When data is collected from the data subject, required information shall be provided at the time of collection.
requirements.recommendation_text: Display Article 13 information at every personal data collection point.

requirements.requirement_text: Data subjects have the right to obtain confirmation and access to their personal data being processed.
requirements.expected_evidence: DSAR procedure, request tracking, response templates
requirements.recommendation_text: Establish a DSAR intake and fulfillment process within 30-day SLA.

requirements.requirement_text: Data subjects have the right to obtain erasure of personal data without undue delay where grounds apply.
requirements.expected_evidence: Deletion procedure, retention schedule, erasure logs
requirements.recommendation_text: Define erasure workflows across primary systems and backups where feasible.

requirements.requirement_text: The controller shall implement appropriate technical and organizational measures for data protection by design and by default.
requirements.expected_evidence: SDLC privacy requirements, DPIA templates, architecture reviews
requirements.recommendation_text: Integrate privacy requirements into SDLC gates and default to minimal data collection.

requirements.requirement_text: Controllers shall maintain records of processing activities under their responsibility.
requirements.expected_evidence: Record of Processing Activities (ROPA), data flow maps
requirements.recommendation_text: Maintain an Article 30 ROPA updated at least quarterly.

requirements.requirement_text: Controllers and processors shall implement appropriate technical and organizational security measures.
requirements.expected_evidence: Security policy, encryption standards, access controls
requirements.recommendation_text: Implement encryption at rest and in transit for personal data with documented key management.

requirements.requirement_text: Personal data breaches shall be notified to the supervisory authority within 72 hours where feasible.
requirements.expected_evidence: Breach response plan, notification templates, incident register
requirements.recommendation_text: Define 72-hour breach notification workflow with DPO and legal involvement.

requirements.requirement_text: When a breach is likely to result in high risk, data subjects shall be informed without undue delay.
requirements.expected_evidence: Breach communication templates, risk assessment criteria
requirements.recommendation_text: Include data subject notification criteria and templates in the breach response plan.

requirements.requirement_text: A data protection impact assessment shall be carried out where processing is likely to result in high risk.
requirements.expected_evidence: DPIA procedure, completed DPIAs, risk register
requirements.recommendation_text: Screen new processing for DPIA triggers and complete assessments before go-live.

requirements.requirement_text: A Data Protection Officer shall be designated where required by law or organizational practice.
requirements.expected_evidence: DPO appointment letter, contact details, independence documentation
requirements.recommendation_text: Designate a DPO or privacy lead with published contact details and independence.

requirements.requirement_text: Processing by a processor shall be governed by a contract with required Article 28 clauses.
requirements.expected_evidence: Signed DPAs, subprocessor list, vendor assessments
requirements.recommendation_text: Execute Article 28 DPAs with all processors and maintain subprocessor register.

requirements.requirement_text: Transfers of personal data outside the EEA shall only occur with appropriate safeguards.
requirements.expected_evidence: Transfer impact assessments, SCCs, transfer register
requirements.recommendation_text: Document all cross-border transfers with SCCs or adequacy decisions and TIAs.

requirements.requirement_text: Personal data shall be kept in a form permitting identification for no longer than necessary.
requirements.expected_evidence: Retention policy, automated deletion jobs, retention schedule
requirements.recommendation_text: Define retention periods per data category and enforce via automated deletion.
